items.title: Upload a certificate via file upload
Save disabled until a file is attached
Scan a certificate with the camera
View every uploaded document fully offline

flows.title: Recording B — Subscription: signup → trial → gating

items.title: iOS — subscription screen without dashboard flash
Price consistency — paywall vs Apple Pay sheet

__m.purpose: Wave test session 14 Jul 2026 — Certificates + iOS Subscriptions (flow-based, iPhone 15 Pro / iOS 18.5). 26 items in 3 recordings; subscriptions split for sandbox timers. Narrate the item number before each test.

f.steps:
1. Fresh account (no certs)
2. Open the Certificates tab
f.expected: 'No Certificates Yet' secure-wallet message; Add Certificate CTA; Search Certificates field; bottom nav = Certificates / Sea Service Records / Profile.
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates · promote candidate · W-CERT-010

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Use the upload form to add a certificate file
3. Assign it to a category/folder
f.expected: Certificate uploads, appears in the chosen folder, opens in the viewer.
f.notes2: plan P1 (upload forms) · spec §1/§3 · W-CERT-001

f.steps:
1. Add Certificate → fill ALL text fields
2. Attach NO file
3. Observe Save License button
f.expected: Correct behaviour defined: Save enabled with validation, OR intentionally disabled with a visible 'attach a file' hint. (Figma showed Save staying disabled with fields filled — narrate exactly.)
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates edge · POSSIBLE BUG · promote candidate · W-CERT-011

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Choose scan
3. Scan a physical document
4. Save to a folder
f.expected: Scan captures legibly and saves as a document in the chosen folder.
f.notes2: spec §3 (scan/upload) · NOTE: scanner integration is listed P2 in the plan — phase ambiguity, see thinness report · W-CERT-002

f.steps: 1. On populated My Certificates, review the category folders
f.expected: Five categories: Travel Documents, Licensing Certifications, Regulatory Statutory Documents, Industry Required, Other — colour-coded, each opening its own list.
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates design-referee · promote candidate · W-CERT-012

f.steps: 1. Add 3+ certificates to the same category
f.expected: All entries listed and individually openable; count correct.
f.notes2: plan P1 (multiple entries management) · W-CERT-003

f.steps: 1. Browse the certificate wallet home
f.expected: Presentation follows the Apple-Wallet-like card metaphor described in the spec (general check; per-screen Figma validation deferred).
f.notes2: spec §3 · design (Figma pass pending) · W-CERT-009

f.steps: 1. Open a certificate card
f.expected: Full field set; Edit (pencil) + Delete (trash) icons; attached Files with View; a QR code.
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates · promote candidate · W-CERT-013

f.steps: 1. Open an uploaded multi-page certificate
f.expected: Viewer shows all pages, supports zoom/scroll, renders text legibly.
f.notes2: plan P1 (document viewer) · W-CERT-004

f.steps: 1. On the detail screen, use the QR code / verification link
f.expected: In-app browser opens the verification URL showing 'Document status: VALID', with back/forward/reload. (Backend-dependent — mark Unverifiable if server unreachable.)
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates · Unverifiable-likely · promote candidate · W-CERT-014

f.steps: 1. Use Search Certificates with a full term, then a partial term
f.expected: Matching certificates surface; non-matches excluded; clear empty-result state.
f.notes2: new this session · figma:certificates · promote candidate · W-CERT-015

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Use export/share
f.expected: Document exports (share sheet / file) intact and readable outside the app. (Formats unspecified — see thinness report.)
f.notes2: spec §1 (export/transact) · W-CERT-007

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Print via the OS print flow
f.expected: Print preview correct; full document prints.
f.notes2: spec §1 (print in case something gets lost) · W-CERT-008

f.steps:
1. Delete a certificate
2. Check the folder and total counts
f.expected: Certificate removed from folder; no orphan entries; viewer no longer opens it.
f.notes2: kit:offline-sync #4 (delete exists) · claude:edge for online case · W-CERT-006

f.steps:
1. Precondition: several documents uploaded across different folders
2. Enable airplane mode (zero connectivity)
3. Force-quit the app, then cold-start it
4. Open each folder and view every uploaded document
f.expected: Every document opens and renders completely with zero connectivity, including after force-quit + cold start. STOP-SHIP if broken.
f.notes2: INVARIANT · spec §1 (offline promise) · kit:offline-sync · W-INV-001

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Brand-new user: full signup (name, email verify, onboarding)
f.expected: Subscription screen appears directly — NO flash of dashboard/certificates first.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-8 · W-SUB-I08

f.steps: 1. On the paywall, review layout
f.expected: Header 'Stay Contract-Ready. Advance Your Career.'; benefit bullets; Monthly $5/mo vs Yearly $50/yr (16% off, pre-selected); '30 Days Free' banner; 'Start 30 days trial' CTA; 'Recover Subscription'; Apple auto-renew legal + Manage subscriptions.
f.notes2: new this session · figma:subscription design-referee · promote candidate · W-SUB-I09

f.steps:
1. From paywall tap Start 30 days trial
2. Read the Apple Pay sheet amount
3. Compare to the paywall's $5 / $50
f.expected: Apple Pay sheet price matches the selected plan. (Figma showed the sheet reading '10 $' against a $50/yr paywall — verify live and narrate the exact figure.)
f.notes2: new this session · figma:subscription edge · POSSIBLE BUG · promote candidate · W-SUB-I10

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Sign up as a new user, complete onboarding
3. On the subscription screen tap 'Start 30 days trial'
4. Confirm in the Apple payment sheet
f.expected: Sheet shows 'Free Trial'; purchase succeeds; app navigates to dashboard.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-1 · sandbox: 1mo≈5min, 12 renewals max · W-SUB-I01

f.steps: 1. With a trialing account, then an active one, use certificates + service records
f.expected: All gated features fully usable in both states.
f.notes2: plan P2 (subscription gating) · kit quick reference · W-SUB-G01

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Subscribe
3. Sign out and back in (or reinstall)
4. Tap 'Restore Purchases'
f.expected: Subscription restored; access granted.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-6 · W-SUB-I06

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Complete W-SUB-I01
3. Wait for the trial period to lapse (~5 min sandbox)
f.expected: Auto-renews to paid; full access retained; backend status trialing → active.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-2 · W-SUB-I02

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Start a fresh trial
3. Sandbox Account > Manage > Subscriptions → cancel
f.expected: Access until trial end, then paywall redirect.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-3 · W-SUB-I03

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Subscribe past trial
3. Cancel via sandbox Manage Subscriptions
4. Wait for period end (~5 min)
f.expected: Access until period end, then paywall.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-4 · W-SUB-I04

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Subscribe monthly
3. Change to yearly via sandbox Manage Subscriptions
f.expected: Plan changes at next renewal.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-7 · W-SUB-I07

f.steps:
1. Precondition: sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app via TestFlight
2. Subscribe and let it renew 12 times (~60 min)
f.expected: Expires automatically after 12 renewals → paywall.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-5 · W-SUB-I05
